title: fix(core): respect sub-agent defaultOptions.memory when called as tool
summary: Fixes a bug where parent agents unconditionally override a sub-agent's defaultOptions.memory config when calling it as a tool. The fix checks if the sub-agent has its own memory config before applying parent-derived thread/resource IDs. Changes are minimal: 2 lines modified in agent.ts (both generate and stream paths) plus 3 new tests and a changeset.
maintainerNotes: Approved by @wardpeet on 2026-01-24. However, the PR now has merge conflicts and CI checks are stuck in pending state (likely due to conflicts preventing them from running). The code change is small and well-scoped: it adds a guard (`!subAgentHasOwnMemoryConfig`) before the parent overrides sub-agent memory in both the generate and stream code paths. Tests cover sub-agents with object defaultOptions, no defaultOptions, and function-based defaultOptions. Author needs to rebase/resolve conflicts before this can be merged.
